- Try to run as much commands yourself as possible. I find it annoying when you tell me "Now run xyz in the terminal"
- We're working on a complicated problem here and already had several sessions without reaching the goal. We got close but you started walking around in circles, trying things that turned out not to work already in previous attempts. I want you to keep track of the attempts in a logfile and whenever you come up with a new idea, check with the previous attempts first whether it is feasible. It could of course be that the code has changed compared to previous attempts and you want to retry an old approach. That's perfectly fine but check with me first.
- This arrayview application needs to work in several settings:
  - On a local PC using CLI, python scripts, jupyter sessions, julia sessions (using PythonCall). It supports a native (py)webview window and browser tabs. When running from within visual studio code, browser tabs should default to vscode simplebrowser tabs. In jupyter notebooks it should run inline by default.
  - Getting vscode to open simplebrowser tabs required - according to your previous attempts - a vscode extension that auto-installs upon calling arrayview. While this is a nice approach, it's handling in python needs to be more robust. Sometimes you get stuck because an older version of the extension is still active. And sometimes when iterating on the code things break. As we speak, its no longer working even though it was working before and now when I call arrayview from within a vscode terminal it opens my system browser. So please be careful and try to make it as robust as possible
  - When connected through a remote (typically my linux pc at work) that has an open vscode tunnel I also want to be able to use arrayview. You tried the vscode extension approach but could not get that working as of yet with the remote. You said something about there not being a marketplace UUID and that it therefore failed to load the extension, i dunno the details. Instead, you found out that there is a remote.portsAttributes setting where it can autoForward ports? I think at some point you got it working but now it no longer does. 
  - In some iterations when i started arrayview from my laptop in the vscode window that's running on my remote through the tunnel, it would open the viewer on the actual remote and not inside of vscode on my laptop. That's of course not the behaviour I want so when you spot a listener to the port (is that the correct term?) it's not guaranteed that things are running as they should
- I aim for this app to be used widely by many colleagues in research. The experience needs to be smooth. Right now we have a nice solution already that it closes gracefully with a message in the terminal but sometimes I still have to run ./kill_arrayview.sh. I really don't want users to have to manually kill processes.
